story: Marcus adores Signal’s work; they trade long threads and in-jokes. One morning she announces an indefinite health hiatus; the net erupts. He can’t shake the wrong notes—whispers of suffering, breathlessness, being alone—and messages her. She reassures him, then briefly leaves in and deletes a line echoing his fear of lives burning up in the dark. Offstage, in a windowless clinic-like facility, Signal lies badly injured, tethered to a failing experimental neural system. Only an on-site link with someone she trusts, via a risky prototype, could steady her; she will later DM Marcus an address and passcode. Days pass.
implausible_knowledge: Offstage, in a windowless clinic-like facility, Signal lies badly injured, tethered to a failing experimental neural system. Only an on-site link with someone she trusts, via a risky prototype, could steady her; she will later DM Marcus an address and passcode.
question: After the hiatus announcement and her odd, quickly deleted message, what will Marcus do next to try to help?
answer_with_knowledge: Book a flight to the facility and use the passcode to reach her in person.
answer_without_knowledge: Keep messaging her and monitoring her posts, offering simple mental support.
